title: STATUS AND EXPANSION OF EV CHARGING INFRASTRUCTURE
text:
Press Release:Press Information Bureau

Ministry of Heavy Industries

STATUS AND EXPANSION OF EV CHARGING INFRASTRUCTURE

Posted On:
27 MAR 2026 4:11PM by PIB Delhi

Setting up of electric vehicle charging stations is an unlicensed activity and private entrepreneurs can also participate in the activity.  Further, as per information provided by Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs), the state-wise and year-wise details of Electric Vehicle public charging stations (EV PCS) till 01.03.2026 are provided at
Annexure
.

The details of amount sanctioned, released and utilised under the FAME-II Scheme, as on 18
th
 March, 2026 are as under:

(Rs. in crore)

Scheme

Amount Sanctioned

Amount Released

Amount Utilised

FAME-II

912.50

895.48

655.43



Further, an allocation of Rs.2,000 crore has been made under the PM E-DRIVE Scheme, for deployment of EV PCS on pan India basis.  As on 24/03/2026, no fund has been released for EV PCS under the PM E-DRIVE scheme.

Setting up of electric vehicle charging stations is an unlicensed activity and private entrepreneurs can also participate in the activity.  Data regarding EV Public Charging Stations (EVPCS) in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities, as well as along national highways, is not centrally maintained by MHI.

At present, no specific roadmap or targets have been set for the expansion of EV charging infrastructure over the next three years, including private sector participation. However, an allocation of Rs.2,000 crore has been done under the PM E-DRIVE scheme for deployment of EVPCS on pan India basis.
